ref: “I will bless you and give you such a large family, that someday your descendants will be more numerous than the stars in the sky or the grains of sand along the seashore. They will defeat their enemies and take over the cities where their enemies live.
text: m làá bìin̄ a-boon tikpil. M làá bùtī a-maaliŋu ŋú nīn wīikí kii iŋmalbijaa nín kpátì kutaa pu puee, kí nīn wīikí kii tiŋku gbandi bulee. A-naantiibi di làá nīn pée pɔ̄ɔǹ bi-nannanliibi.
translated: The dog is very clever. The dog is a good hunter and can catch a monkey in the forest, and can catch a monkey in the village, and the monkey cannot escape. The hunter's trap is what catches the monkey.

ref: Abram obeyed the Lord. Then he cut the animals in half and laid the two halves of each animal opposite each other on the ground. But he did not cut the doves and pigeons in half.
text: Ní Abram nyàabìń an waŋkutee ní ki cúú-tì ki gífì ki nābitì mili mili, ní ki dū aginee ki bíl̀ ki túĺ tɔb. See kinanjilii nì dibeenanjipɔmbil ní waa gífì ki nābitì mili.
translated: And Abram traveled through the land to the place of Shechem, as far as the oak of Moreh, and the Canaanites were in the land at that time, and the Lord appeared to Abram and said to him, "To your descendants I will give this land."

ref: The older daughter named her son Moab, and he is the ancestor of the Moabites.
text: Ní ukpil màl̄ kibininjabiki ní ki pú-kì diyindi yii Moowaab. Úǹ di sá díǹ pu nee Moowaab yab naanja.
translated: The people of the village were very happy when the Moowaab came. They gave them food and drink when the Moowaab arrived at their place.

ref: I promise every living creature that the earth and those living on it will never again be destroyed by a flood.
text: Dipoondi din m pōoǹ-nee sɔ̀. Maa làá ti yàntī nnyiŋgbeen ń dāá kúntí tiwan tin kɔkɔ cáá kufoouee tiba kitiŋ pu.»
translated: When I am in the house of my father, I do not know all the things that my father does, but when I go out, I will know everything.

ref: The Lord asked Abraham, “Why did Sarah laugh? Does she doubt that she can have a child in her old age?
text: Ní ti-Dindaan bí Abraam yii: «Ba pu ní Saara láń ki bí yii an kaa sá tiŋman, úǹ púĺ waa ti ŋūǹ kí màal̀ kibiki?
translated: Is anything too hard for the Lord? At the appointed time I will return to you, and Sarah will have a son
